title: Product Manager at a Startup – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Startup product manager responsibilities, required skills, and resume keywords. How to position your experience for fast-moving PM roles.
intro: Product managers at startups wear many hats: roadmap ownership, customer discovery, and working closely with engineering and design. This guide outlines typical responsibilities, skills, and resume keywords so you can present your experience in a way that resonates with startup hiring managers and ATS.
responsibilities: Define product vision, strategy, and roadmap in alignment with business goals
Gather and prioritize customer and stakeholder requirements
Write clear specs, user stories, and acceptance criteria for engineering
Work with design and engineering in agile cycles to ship features
Analyze usage data and feedback to iterate on product decisions
Communicate roadmap and trade-offs to leadership and cross-functional teams
requiredSkills: Data-informed decision making (metrics, A/B tests, user research)
Technical fluency to work with engineers on scope and feasibility
salaryRange: Varies widely; early-stage often $90,000–$140,000 base plus equity; growth-stage can be higher.
body:
Startup PM roles value ownership and outcomes over formal process. On your resume, lead with impact: features shipped, metrics improved, and how you influenced strategy or prioritization. Use concrete numbers (e.g. adoption rates, revenue impact, user growth).

Tailoring your resume to each startup's stage and product area will help you stand out. WadeCV can help you reframe your existing PM experience for startup-style job descriptions so your strongest wins are front and centre.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on process (e.g. 'ran agile') without business outcomes
Generic bullets that could apply to any company
Skipping metrics; startups care about growth and impact
interviewTips: Prepare product case studies and prioritisation frameworks
Be ready to discuss trade-offs and how you used data
Show you can work with limited resources and ambiguity

faq.question: What do startup PM hiring managers look for?
faq.answer: Evidence of ownership (you shipped X, it led to Y), comfort with ambiguity, and ability to work closely with engineering and design. Metrics and outcomes matter more than job title or company size.

title: Data Analyst in Healthcare – Job Description & Resume Tips
metaDescription: Healthcare data analyst responsibilities, skills, salary ranges, and resume keywords to land roles in providers, payers, and health tech.
intro: Data analysts in healthcare help organizations make sense of clinical, operational, and financial data. Roles exist at providers, payers, pharma, and health tech companies. This guide covers typical job requirements and how to tailor your resume for these positions.
responsibilities: Build and maintain dashboards and reports for clinical, operational, or financial metrics
Clean, transform, and analyze large datasets (claims, EMR, surveys)
Support ad hoc analyses and present findings to clinical and business stakeholders
Ensure data quality and compliance with HIPAA and internal policies
Collaborate with IT, clinical teams, and leadership to define reporting needs
requiredSkills: SQL and experience with relational databases and data warehouses
Visualization tools (e.g. Tableau, Power BI, Looker)
Statistical literacy and basic scripting (Python or R often preferred)
Understanding of healthcare data (claims, EMR, coding) and regulatory context
Ability to explain technical results to non-technical audiences
salaryRange: Often $65,000–$110,000 depending on experience, organization, and location.
body: Healthcare data roles value both technical skills and domain awareness. On your resume, highlight experience with healthcare datasets, compliance considerations, and how your analyses drove decisions for clinicians or operations. Quantify impact where you can (e.g. reports used by X teams, time saved, or improvements in key metrics). Tailoring your resume to each employer's focus—clinical quality, revenue cycle, population health, or product analytics—will improve your fit. Use WadeCV to align your background with specific healthcare data analyst job descriptions.

faq.question: What skills do healthcare data analyst roles look for?
faq.answer: SQL, visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI), and often Python or R. Domain knowledge of healthcare data (claims, EMR, HIPAA) and the ability to explain results to non-technical stakeholders are also important.

title: Customer Success Manager Job Description & Resume Keywords
metaDescription: Customer success manager responsibilities, skills, salary ranges, and resume keywords. How to tailor your resume for CSM roles in SaaS and tech.
intro: Customer success managers focus on adoption, retention, and growth of existing customers. The role is critical in SaaS and subscription businesses. This guide outlines typical job requirements and how to present your experience so recruiters and ATS recognise your fit.
responsibilities: Onboard new customers and drive time-to-value and adoption
Monitor health scores, usage, and risk indicators; run proactive outreach
Conduct business reviews and identify expansion or upsell opportunities
Collaborate with support, product, and sales to resolve issues and share feedback
Renew contracts and reduce churn through relationship and value delivery
Document processes and contribute to playbooks and best practices
requiredSkills: Experience with CRM (e.g. Salesforce), support tools, and product usage data
Ability to understand customer goals and map solutions to outcomes
Organisation and project management across multiple accounts
Empathy and resilience when handling escalations
salaryRange: Often $70,000–$120,000 base plus variable compensation, depending on segment and company.
body: CSM roles reward outcomes: retention rates, expansion revenue, and improved adoption or NPS. On your resume, lead with metrics—renewal rates, revenue retained or expanded, number of accounts, and feedback scores. Show that you can own the relationship and work across internal teams. Tailoring your resume to the company's segment (SMB, mid-market, enterprise) and industry will help. WadeCV can help you align your customer-facing experience with CSM job descriptions and highlight your strongest impact.

faq.question: What metrics should I put on a customer success manager resume?
faq.answer: Renewal rate, retention, NPS or CSAT, expansion revenue, number of accounts managed, and adoption or usage metrics. Lead with outcomes that show you drove retention and growth.

title: Frontend Engineer Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Frontend engineer job description: responsibilities, skills, salary, and resume keywords for React, Vue, and modern web roles.
intro: Frontend engineers build the user-facing side of web and mobile applications. Demand is strong for developers who combine strong JavaScript/TypeScript skills with an eye for UX and performance. This guide breaks down typical job requirements and resume keywords so you can tailor your application.
responsibilities: Build responsive, accessible UIs with modern frameworks (React, Vue, Angular)
Collaborate with design and backend to implement features and APIs
Optimise performance (Core Web Vitals, bundle size)
Write maintainable code and tests; participate in code review
Stay current with browser APIs and front-end tooling
requiredSkills: Testing (Jest, Cypress, etc.); build tools (Webpack, Vite)
Collaboration and attention to UX detail
salaryRange: Often $90,000–$180,000+ depending on level and location.
body:
Frontend roles reward both technical depth and user impact. On your resume, highlight projects with scale (page views, users) or performance gains, and list the frameworks and tools that match the job. Tailor keywords to the posting—many roles emphasise React or Vue specifically.

WadeCV can help you align your frontend experience with each job description and surface your strongest bullets.

Model data and work with relational and/or NoSQL databases
Ensure reliability, security, and scalability
Debug production issues and participate in on-call

requiredSkills: Proficiency in one or more backend languages (e.g. Python, Java, Go, Node.js)
SQL and database design; caching and message queues
salaryRange: Often $100,000–$200,000+ depending on level and location.
body:
Backend resumes should show system ownership and impact: APIs you built, scale you handled, and how you improved reliability or performance. Use metrics (throughput, latency, uptime) where possible. Match the language and stack in the job description.

WadeCV can help you tailor your backend experience to each role and highlight your strongest technical outcomes.

body:
DevOps roles value automation, reliability, and scale. On your resume, lead with outcomes: deployment frequency, uptime, or cost/speed improvements. List the tools and clouds that match the job. Tailoring your resume to each company's stack will improve your fit.

title: Entry-Level Software Engineer Job Description & Resume Guide

Fix bugs and implement small features
Learn codebase and team practices
Collaborate with peers and ask for feedback
Document and communicate progress

Basic version control (Git); debugging and testing
Ability to read and understand existing code
salaryRange: Often $70,000–$120,000 depending on location and company.
body:
Without long work history, lead with projects, internships, and relevant coursework. Use clear bullet points that describe what you built and what you learned. Match the language in the job description (languages, tools) and keep formatting clean for ATS.

title: Scrum Master Job Description & Resume Guide

responsibilities: Facilitate sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives, and demos
Remove blockers and shield the team from distractions
Coach team on agile practices and continuous improvement
Track velocity and metrics; report to stakeholders
Support product owner on backlog refinement
requiredSkills: Strong knowledge of Scrum and often Kanban
Understanding of software development and delivery
Communication with technical and non-technical stakeholders
salaryRange: Often $80,000–$130,000 depending on experience and location.
body:
Scrum master resumes should show impact on delivery and team health: cycle time, release frequency, or team satisfaction. Highlight facilitation and coaching, not just ceremony management. Tailor to the organisation's agile maturity and any mentioned frameworks.

title: Senior Product Manager Job Description & Resume Guide

WadeCV can help you align your senior PM experience with job descriptions and surface your strongest leadership and impact.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on tactics; senior roles need strategy and scope
Generic bullets that don't show level of ownership
interviewTips: Be ready to discuss trade-offs and how you influenced without authority
Show how you developed others and scaled product practice

faq.question: What do senior PM hiring managers look for?

title: UX Designer Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: UX designer job description: responsibilities, skills, and resume keywords for product design roles.

responsibilities: Conduct user research and usability testing

salaryRange: Often $90,000–$160,000+ depending on level and location.
body: UX resumes should lead with user and business impact: conversion, satisfaction, efficiency. Show research and design process and how it drove outcomes. Tailor to the product type (B2B, B2C, internal tools). WadeCV can help you align your UX experience with job descriptions.
commonMistakes: Portfolio-only focus; resume should stand alone with impact
Vague bullets without metrics or scope

faq.question: How do I show UX impact on a resume?
faq.answer: Use metrics: conversion rate changes, task completion time, satisfaction scores, or support ticket reduction. Describe the problem, your approach, and the outcome in 1–2 lines per project.

title: Data Scientist Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Data scientist job description: ML, analytics, and resume keywords for DS and ML engineer roles.
intro: Data scientists build models, run experiments, and turn data into decisions. Roles span analytics, ML, and research. This guide covers typical job requirements and how to tailor your resume for data science positions.
responsibilities: Build and deploy models for prediction, recommendation, or classification
Design and analyse A/B tests and experiments

Present findings to technical and non-technical stakeholders
requiredSkills: Python or R; SQL; statistics and ML fundamentals
ML frameworks (e.g. scikit-learn, TensorFlow); feature engineering
Software engineering practices where models are productionised
salaryRange: Often $120,000–$200,000+ depending on level and location.
body: Data science resumes should show impact: model performance, experiment results, or decisions influenced. Include technical stack and scale (data size, users affected). Tailor to the role (more ML vs more analytics). WadeCV can help you align your DS experience with job descriptions.
commonMistakes: Too much methodology, not enough impact

faq.question: How many technical details should be on a data scientist resume?
faq.answer: Enough to show depth (language, frameworks, methods) but lead with impact: what the model or analysis did for the business, and at what scale.

title: QA Engineer Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: QA engineer job description: testing, automation, and resume keywords for quality assurance roles.
intro: QA engineers ensure product quality through manual and automated testing. The role combines technical skill with attention to detail. This guide covers typical job requirements and resume keywords for QA and SDET roles.
responsibilities: Design and execute test plans and cases
Build and maintain automated test suites (e.g. Selenium, Cypress)
Report bugs and work with development on fixes
Participate in release and regression testing
Improve test coverage and CI integration
requiredSkills: Programming (e.g. Python, JavaScript) for test code
API and database basics for test data
salaryRange: Often $70,000–$130,000 depending on level and location.
body: QA resumes should show impact: coverage improved, bugs caught, release quality. Include automation stack and scale (number of tests, environments). Tailor to the product type and stack in the job description. WadeCV can help you align your QA experience with job descriptions.
commonMistakes: Only listing test types; show outcomes and scale

faq.question: Should I list manual vs automated testing on my QA resume?
faq.answer: Yes. Show both—manual for exploratory and edge cases, automation for regression and scale. Quantify where you can (e.g. 'Maintained 500+ automated tests').
